interview_description: This interview helps us understand your story preferences so we can generate personalized story synopses for you

questions.question: Which genres interest you the most?
questions.subtitle: Select up to 3 genres to focus on
questions.purpose: This helps us provide genre-specific tools, prompts, and story elements tailored to your interests.

options.label: Sci-fi
options.tooltip: Stories set in the future or alternate realities, featuring advanced technology, space exploration, or scientific concepts

options.label: Romance
options.tooltip: Stories centered on romantic relationships and emotional connections between characters

options.label: Fantasy
options.tooltip: Stories with magical elements, mythical creatures, and imaginary worlds beyond reality

options.label: Mystery
options.tooltip: Stories focused on solving puzzles, crimes, or unexplained events through investigation

options.label: Horror
options.tooltip: Stories designed to frighten, unsettle, or create suspense through supernatural or psychological elements

options.label: Adventure
options.tooltip: Action-packed stories featuring quests, exploration, and exciting challenges

options.label: Historical Fiction
options.tooltip: Stories set in the past that blend historical facts with fictional characters and events

options.label: Thriller/Suspense
options.tooltip: Fast-paced stories with high stakes, danger, and constant tension

options.label: Literary Fiction
options.tooltip: Character-driven stories focusing on theme, style, and psychological depth

options.label: Dystopian
options.tooltip: Stories set in imagined societies with oppressive control or post-apocalyptic settings

options.label: Young Adult (YA)
options.tooltip: Stories for teens and young adults, often featuring coming-of-age themes

options.label: Urban Fantasy
options.tooltip: Fantasy stories set in modern cities where magic exists alongside everyday life

questions.question: What tone are you aiming for in your story?
questions.purpose: This helps our AI adjust its suggestions to match your desired mood and atmosphere.

questions.question: What type of character will be central to your story?
questions.subtitle: Character 1
questions.purpose: This helps us understand the main character type you want to focus on in your story.

options.label: Protagonist
options.tooltip: The main character whose journey we follow throughout the story

options.label: Antagonist
options.tooltip: The primary opposing force or villain that creates conflict for the main character

options.label: Anti-hero
options.tooltip: A flawed main character who lacks traditional heroic qualities but is still sympathetic

options.label: Supporting Character
options.tooltip: An important secondary character who helps or influences the main story

options.label: Ensemble Character
options.tooltip: One of multiple equally important characters in a group-focused story

options.label: Mentor Figure
options.tooltip: A wise character who guides and teaches the main character

options.label: Comic Relief
options.tooltip: A character who provides humor and lightens tense moments

options.label: Love Interest
options.tooltip: A character central to a romantic subplot or main plot

questions.question: What would you like to name this character?
questions.subtitle: Character 1 Name
questions.purpose: Give your character a name to make them feel more real and personal to your story.

questions.question: Tell us about this character's background
questions.subtitle: Character 1 Background
questions.purpose: This background information helps create story samples that feel authentic to your character.
questions.placeholder: Describe their personality, history, motivations, appearance, or anything that makes them unique...

questions.question: What type of character will be your second character?
questions.subtitle: Character 2
questions.purpose: This helps us understand the second main character type you want to include in your story.

options.label: Protagonist
options.tooltip: The main character whose journey we follow throughout the story

options.label: Antagonist
options.tooltip: The primary opposing force or villain that creates conflict for the main character

options.label: Anti-hero
options.tooltip: A flawed main character who lacks traditional heroic qualities but is still sympathetic

options.label: Supporting Character
options.tooltip: An important secondary character who helps or influences the main story

options.label: Ensemble Character
options.tooltip: One of multiple equally important characters in a group-focused story

options.label: Mentor Figure
options.tooltip: A wise character who guides and teaches the main character

options.label: Comic Relief
options.tooltip: A character who provides humor and lightens tense moments

options.label: Love Interest
options.tooltip: A character central to a romantic subplot or main plot

questions.question: What would you like to name this second character?
questions.subtitle: Character 2 Name
questions.purpose: Give your second character a name to make them feel more real and personal to your story.

questions.question: Tell us about this second character's background
questions.subtitle: Character 2 Background
questions.purpose: This background information helps create story samples that feel authentic to your second character.
questions.placeholder: Describe their personality, history, motivations, appearance, or anything that makes them unique...

questions.question: What elements would you like to see in your story?
questions.subtitle: Check all that apply
questions.purpose: These elements help us generate diverse story synopses that include the aspects you're most interested in exploring.
